claim: Fiscal contraction has an asymmetrically higher multiplier (1.2-1.8x) than fiscal expansion (0.6-0.9x) at current debt levels
needs: U.S.-specific evidence on asymmetry. Cross-country panel testing whether monetary-sovereign issuers exhibit the same asymmetry as Eurozone members without monetary sovereignty.

claim: 2Y-SPX correlation is -0.25 to -0.35 while 30Y-SPX is +0.05 to +0.15, reflecting monetary dominance at front end and fiscal supply dominance at long end
needs: Historical analysis of maturity-dependent stock-bond correlations across multiple fiscal and monetary regimes. Current real-time data validation.

claim: Equity-implied HY spreads are 50-75bp wider than actual spreads, reflecting differential fiscal optimism between credit and equity investors
needs: Decomposition of credit-equity basis into technical vs. fundamental drivers. Historical analysis of whether the basis widens before fiscal stress episodes.

claim: Fiscal austerity that eliminates biofuel tax credits while preserving volume mandates creates market distortions
needs: Political probability assessment of targeted credit elimination. Historical analysis of blender behavior under margin compression.

claim: Under fiscal dominance, stock-bond correlation flips positive, duration becomes risk factor, and quality factor premium expands 2-3x
needs: Decomposition of 2022 stock-bond correlation into fiscal vs. monetary components. Cross-country analysis of factor returns during confirmed fiscal dominance episodes.

claim: The Fama-French HML factor has ~1-2% incremental annual return attributable to fiscal policy after controlling for cycle and monetary policy
needs: Out-of-sample testing in European and Japanese markets. Proper multi-factor attribution controlling for all correlated macro variables simultaneously.

claim: Fiscal multipliers are overstated when food prices are elevated due to import leakage
needs: State-dependent multiplier estimation conditioning on agricultural price regime. EM-specific empirical work.

claim: IRA rollback under DOGE would remove 800kt-1.2Mt of incremental copper demand from 2028-2032 balance sheets
needs: Political probability assessment of IRA credit survival. Alternative demand sources that could partially offset IRA rollback.

claim: A U.S. sovereign credit downgrade below AA would trigger forced selling and collateral substitution through Basel III risk weighting, central bank repo eligibility, and insurance capital requirements
needs: Quantification of forced selling from regulatory reclassification. Analysis of whether the 2011 downgrade experience is informative for a further downgrade scenario.
